title: Durable Long-Running Work and Operations · Operations Recall
summary: Operate a multi-hour or multi-day agent task through interruptions. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a durable runbook and restartable workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate durable orchestration state from ephemeral model turns.
Use scheduled checks or heartbeats only when timing or external state requires them.
Wait or subscribe for expected external changes without classifying unchanged state as failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.instruction: Order the Durable Execution evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Durable Execution assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Separate durable orchestration state from ephemeral model turns. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops long running. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Durable Execution, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a durable runbook and restartable workflow.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Keeping one process alive makes a workflow durable. This fails the Durable Execution contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Operator Handoff case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: loops long running. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Operators only need a binary running indicator.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Expose current phase, next wake condition, authority, costs, and safe interventions. Artifact under review: a durable runbook and restartable workflow.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Operator Handoff, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Operator Handoff applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Expose current phase, next wake condition, authority, costs, and safe interventions.
